title: albuca sordida
intro: Albuca sordida, also known as the spotted albuca or yellow sea squill, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its health benefits are said to include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a remedy for snake bites and other poisonous stings. In herbal preparations, Albuca sordida is often used in the form of a tincture or infusion, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
